channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Robinhood’s startup fund stumbles in NYSE debut
item.description: The fund currently offers retail investors exposure to eight startups, including Mercor, Ramp, and Stripe, with plans to expand its portfolio.

item.title: ‘No one ever wants the knock on the door to be from the FBI’ — Friends Like These: The Murder of Skylar Neese expert weighs in on why true crime makes us so anxious
item.description: New Hulu true crime series Friends Like These: The Murder of Skylar Neese explores deceit at its cruelest — and one FBI expert says it's 'natural' for fans to be worried too.

item.title: Vivo teases the most powerful camera phone ever with a 400mm telephoto lens accessory, but it is just a gimmick?
item.description: Vivo’s X300 Ultra phone has a 400mm lens attachment for incredible zoom capabilities — and another accessory could be joining it soon for 'general photography'.

item.title: There's a sneaky way to watch Outlander 2026 for free
item.description: Here's our guide on a super simple way to stream and watch Outlander 2026 for free from anywhere in the world, as the historical fantasy reaches its conclusion.

item.title: Sick of slow PS5 game downloads? This simple trick could save you a whole lot of time
item.description: This simple change cut my PS5 game download times dramatically — here’s how you could save yourself a bunch of time too.

item.title: I tested the affordable GoPro Lit Hero — and it might be the brand's most confusing action cam yet
item.description: The GoPro Lit Hero is one of the smallest action cameras around, but the slimmed-down spec might have required a few too many compromises, even at the affordable price.

item.title: Chinese researchers develop the first semi-solid-state EV battery with a massive 620-mile range — but questions remain
item.description: Chinese researchers are working on semi-solid-state battery technology that could result in an EV range in excess of 1,000 miles.

item.title: Anthropic launches Claude Marketplace, giving enterprises access to Claude-powered tools from Replit, GitLab, Harvey and more
item.description: San Francisco startup Anthropic continues to ship new AI products and services at a blistering pace, despite a messy ongoing dispute with the U.S. Department of War.Today, the company announced Claude Marketplace, a new offering that lets enterprises with an existing Anthropic spend commitment apply part of it toward tools and applications powered by Anthropic's Claude models but made and offered by external partners including GitLab, Harvey, Lovable, Replit, Rogo and Snowflake.According to Anthropic’s Claude Marketplace FAQ, the program is designed to simplify procurement and consolidate AI s...
